overall_justification: This edition of Correio da Manhã advocates strongly for the establishment of a Caixa de Conversão, but not at the high, orthodox rate of 27d. It explicitly criticizes this approach as utopian and ruinous for the current generation. Instead, it champions a more pragmatic approach, drawing direct inspiration from Argentina's successful model, emphasizing the importance of a fixed rate (even if lower than 27d) for stability and economic progress.

overall_justification: This edition of Correio da Manhã expresses strong opposition to the Taubaté Convênio, focusing on its proposal for a Caixa de Conversão backed by a large foreign loan.  The paper frames this as a dangerous deviation from fiscal responsibility and a threat to Brazil's monetary stability, aligning with core tenets of the orthodox perspective.  The criticism is prominent, appearing on the front page and in a dedicated follow-up article.
